name: Stuffed Chicken with Nutty Cinnamon Rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a wok or frying pan. Add the garlic, onion, fennel seeds, chili pepper, and cinnamon. Stir fry until aromatic.
2. Add the ground pork and stir fry until the pork has changed color.
3. Stir in the peanuts and rice, stirring until combined.
4. Add the coconut cream and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10 minutes. Remove the lid and stir in the mint; let cool.
5. Spoon the stuffing mixture into the cavities of the chickens, forcing any remaining stuffing under the skin around the necks. Secure the cavity openings with toothpicks or sew them closed. Secure the legs with kitchen string and tuck the wings behind the backs.
6. In a large saucepan that can fit both chickens, combine the extra coconut cream, water, fish sauce, and curry powder. Bring to a boil, add the chickens, and simmer covered for about 45 minutes or until cooked through (turn once during cooking).
7. Remove the chickens from the pan and keep warm. Return the pan to the heat and simmer uncovered over medium heat for about 15 minutes or until thickened slightly and reduced to about 1 1/2 cups liquid. The mixture will form a light sauce.
8. Using sharp kitchen scissors, cut down the centers of the chickens. Serve with the sauce, sprinkling sliced chilies and extra chopped peanuts if desired.
